checked_queries: duplicate async jobs prune stale pool
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing self-healing behavior: active duplicate scheduled or regular async jobs are not both cached as InvalidAsync and pruned from the transaction pool by original transaction hash.
related_but_insufficient_code: For synthesized scheduled obligations the builder checks job_exists_and_active and continues on SkipActiveJob, but it does not mark any async detection cache entry invalid or prune a transaction-pool original hash.
The normal payload builder marks invalid for gas/size/permanent invalidity cases, not for active AsyncJobTracker duplicate jobs.

notes: Confirmed gap; active-job skip exists but prune/cache side effects do not.
rationale: The payload builder self-healing behavior applies to both scheduled and regular async duplicates when AsyncJobTracker already has a job for the transaction.
statement: At current tip, when payload building detects that a scheduled or regular async transaction already has a job in AsyncJobTracker, it must skip the transaction, mark it invalid in the async detection cache, and prune the original transaction hash from the transaction pool.
suggested_falsification_tests: Mock job_exists to return true for one scheduled async transaction and one regular async transaction, run payload building, and assert each transaction is skipped, cached as InvalidAsync, and passed to prune_original_transaction_by_hash exactly once.
summary: Duplicate async-job pruning of stale pool transactions is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Mock job_exists_and_active=true for scheduled and regular async candidates and assert each is skipped, cached InvalidAsync, and removed from txpool; current scheduled path only skips.
title: Duplicate async jobs prune stale pool transactions
